input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
# Or for .NET 9:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Install MAUI (pulls in Android SDK, build tools, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target requires the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The easiest route:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# If you prefer Build Tools only (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add required components:

```powershell
# Modify the installation to include MAUI + Windows App SDK
# (run from an elevated prompt)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and discover paths.

```powershell
# Find where the workload put the Android SDK
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: check the dotnet MAUI manifest for the path
    $androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Android\Sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Discover installed platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
  Select-String "installed"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level if needed (e.g., API 34)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

Set the environment variable so builds find it:

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk
```

## Step 5 — Install an Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"
```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows Target)

```powershell
# Enable Windows Developer Mode (elevated prompt)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Discover available target frameworks in the project
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' .\MauiTestApp.csproj

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

> Adjust `net8.0` → `net9.0` if you installed .NET 9.

## Step 8 — Troubleshooting Commands

```powershell
# Check all workload versions and status
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload repair

# Discover which Android API the current MAUI SDK targets
dotnet new maui --dry-run 2>$null
Select-String 'android' "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\platforms\*\build.prop"

# Check Windows App SDK registration
Get-AppxPackage *WindowsAppRuntime* | Select-Object Name, Version
```

---

**Key order of operations:** .NET SDK → MAUI workload → VS/Build Tools (for WinUI) → Android licenses → Developer Mode → verify with a test build. Each step depends on the previous one completing successfully.
